meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve can't scale projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't scale projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Scaling Student Projects for Profit
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with scaling projects. Learn how to implement SaaS pricing strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate developer case study.
content.answer_block: Scaling issues often stem from misaligned value capture. By implementing a tiered SaaS pricing model integrated with automated usage limits, you transform your project from a free utility into a sustainable business. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to automate the deployment of your subscription logic.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect app, only to realize that as your user base grows, your infrastructure costs and maintenance burden are spiraling out of control. The 'free-forever' trap is the silent killer of student projects; it turns your passion into a liability that prevents you from shipping new features. 

True engineering isn't just about writing code; it's about building systems that sustain themselves. By shifting your mindset toward SaaS pricing, you create a feedback loop that funds your server costs and validates your product's worth. Let's turn your hobby project into a professional-grade SaaS.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, map out your pricing tiers based on usage metrics rather than generic labels. This prevents resource exhaustion by capping 'free' users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project features and suggest three tiered pricing levels based on API usage, storage limits, and concurrent job capacity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a tiered pricing schema for my current database model'

steps.title: Architecting Usage-Based Limits
steps.description: Implement middleware that checks user subscription status before executing expensive functions. This protects your system from abuse.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust middleware function that checks the user's current subscription tier and enforces a request limit per minute.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the API route handlers to include subscription-based access control'

steps.title: Integrating Stripe for SaaS Billing
steps.description: Connect your app to a payment processor to handle subscription webhooks, ensuring your database stays in sync with payment events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Stripe webhook handler that updates the user's subscription status in my PostgreSQL database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the Stripe webhook endpoint and test it against the local dev environment'

steps.title: Automating Deployment with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate the deployment pipeline to a staging environment for testing your new pricing logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment script that validates my pricing infrastructure before pushing to production.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize deployment and run automated tests on the new subscription logic'

steps.title: Observability and Scaling
steps.description: Set up alerts for when users hit their limits to identify if you need to adjust your pricing or optimize your query performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a dashboard query that tracks conversion rates from free to paid tiers based on user activity logs.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add telemetry to track how many users reach their quota limits'

pro_tips: Never offer unlimited usage; always set a hard cap to force a decision point for your users.
Use 'Feature Gating' instead of just 'Access Gating' to make paid tiers feel inherently more valuable.
Implement a grace period logic in your database schema to avoid immediate churn on failed credit card payments.
Always log the 'reason' a user upgrades to help you identify which features are your most profitable hooks.
Use mock billing data in your test suite to ensure your subscription logic doesn't break during peak traffic.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on analyzing user churn and optimizing your onboarding funnel to convert free users into paid customers.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your projects drain your time and money. Start charging for the value you provide and build a sustainable future. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
